steps.title: Use the vertex
steps.content: The parabola $y=-3(x-18)^2+15$ opens downward and has vertex $(18,15)$. Since it opens downward, its greatest possible $y$-value is $15$.

steps.title: Compare the line
steps.content: The horizontal line is $y=18$, which is above the parabola's maximum value of $15$. The two graphs never intersect, so there are zero intersection points. The answer is D.
